scope: PMS-R7 decode attention reopen gate (whole-model leverage, not a rewrite)

model_arch.decode_bound: weight-memory-bound (HBM); weight read dominates the per-token wall

decision_reason: DO_NOT_REOPEN_ATTENTION: (1) realizable whole-decode gain = 0% -- the best available attention route is 68.3% of owned (BELOW owned), and every attention search axis is refuted/exhausted (['attention_combine_fused_lifecycle', 'native_attention_as_default', 'n1b_scalar_address_path', 'occupancy_lds_only_attention_tuning', 'scheduler_only_attention_tuning']), so no candidate can realize the Amdahl ceiling; (2) even the theoretical ceiling is small and ctx-decaying (10.2%@ctx512 -> 3.0%@ctx4096), below the 5.0% reopen bar at the representative long-ctx operating point; the dominant decode wall is the weight-memory-bound FFN/projection (HBM), not attention.
what_would_make_it_active: much longer context with a large KV cache that materially raises the attention tile wall-share
a different KV layout / dtype that raises the KV-read floor relative to weight read
larger Hq/Hkv/Hd (head_dim) increasing attention FLOPs/bytes per token
MoE or a larger FFN-sparsity that shrinks the dense-FFN wall-share so attention dominates
a different GPU with higher HBM bandwidth making the weight read less dominant
a NEW attention primitive premise distinct from the refuted axes (not native-as-default, not combine/fused-lifecycle, not scheduler/occupancy/LDS-only, not N1B scalar-address)
do_not: do not start from 'the native route is slower'
